nodes.label: Crypts of the Fallen Dwarf Kings of Delzoun
Ruins of the Fallen Netherese Empire

edges.desc: serves as the characters' home base during the adventure, where they can rest and get supplies
explore an island that has long been a battlefield in the ancient dragon war
Fighters dare the crypts seeking glory and treasure
Rogues prowl the dark alleyways of teeming cities
Rogues prowl the dark alleyways of teeming cities
Wizards plunder the ruins delving into sinister secrets
Bold adventurers follow tales of strange, glorious, faraway places
This adventure takes place on Stormwreck Isle where daring souls explore
established Dragon's Rest as a safe haven and serves as its leader
lives on Stormwreck Isle in human guise as part of Dragon's Rest
the adventure begins with Runara's sanctuary threatened by dragon conflict
Runara killed a blue dragon at the Clifftop Observatory
Characters explore the Seagrow Caves where Sharruth's grave spawns fire connections
Characters investigate the cursed shipwreck alongside gold dragon bones
Characters visit the Clifftop Observatory where Runara killed a blue dragon
The ship's hold contains a horrible curse within the cursed shipwreck
The characters arrive at locations marked on the map of Stormwreck Isle
encounters the players' ship at sea near Stormwreck Isle
intimidates and extorts the players during their voyage
The players are currently sailing through the Sea of Swords on their journey to Stormwreck Isle
is present in the ship's hold demanding tribute
can be persuaded to accept lower tribute through Charisma checks
Players will attack the Merrow Extortionist first, with characters acting in combat order when players decide to attack
Merrow Extortionist is located on Stormwreck Isle
Players seek out Runara's wisdom and assistance, learning about the dangers facing Stormwreck Isle
three shambling zombie sailors are present at this location
zombies will move toward and attack the characters if they choose to fight
rescues the players by defeating zombies that were attacking them
The characters escape from zombies and move up the path toward Dragon's Rest
is the leader of Dragon's Rest cloister
lives at Dragon's Rest and sells equipment to characters
If characters harm residents, Runara becomes hostile and insists they make amends before dealing with them
Nine kobolds are utterly devoted to Runara and live at Dragon's Rest, sharing her ideals of justice and compassion
Nine kobolds live at Dragon's Rest, working at night and avoiding movement during the day due to sunlight sensitivity
Many bands of kobolds have been drawn to Stormwreck Isle over centuries by the lingering draconic magic
Runara helped Myla recover from wing injuries and now Myla serves as a kobold tinkerer at Dragon's Rest
Myla's wings were badly injured in an attack by Stirge, which she describes as 'hungry, icky, blood-sucking, bat-things'
Myla's brothers, Mek and Minn, now follow Sparkrender, the Blue Dragon Wyrmling in Clifftop Observatory
The kobolds of Dragon's Rest are initially friendly toward the adventurers unless otherwise noted
uses heart cap mushrooms from myconids to create healing potions
worried about being turned away by guardian at sea caves and may ask for help (implied by 'Cloister Quests' reference)
initially friendly toward visitors and eager to share herbalism knowledge
becomes hostile if characters pry into his past and continue pushing after initial indifference
spends her twilight years in reflective contemplation at Dragon's Rest
uses mason's tools to maintain the temple and other areas of the cloister
can shift her attitude to friendly by engaging in conversation on history, ethics, and impact of individual actions
Tarak's cell is next to the vacant cell available to the characters
Varnoth's cell is next to Tarak's cell in the monastic cloister
Myla's cluttered cell is the fourth cell in the monastic cloister
Tarak is one of the cloister residents who shares meals and takes turns cooking in the kitchen/dining room
The players are among the cloister residents who share meals in the kitchen/dining room
Tarak cooks meals for the residents, and the days when Tarak cooks are everyone's favorite
comes to the cloister library to read and discuss various works
helps Runara maintain the temple along with other residents of the cloister
zombies have attacked Blepp the kobold, leaving him with only 2 hit points
zombies are overpowering the unarmed Tarak
characters have an opportunity to fight the three zombies to save Blepp and Tarak
given to the players by Tarak
believes the answer to the shipwreck mystery is located at the Compass Rose wreck
knows Tarak has experience with escaping corruption and violence, and recommends the paladin speak with them
knows Varnoth has experience with escaping corruption and violence, and recommends the paladin speak with them
controls access to the observatory and requires characters to prove themselves worthy before directing them there
fears going to the observatory herself due to old wounds and suspects evil has arisen there
Runara is dedicated to peace and will rescue the players if they become unconscious on the island
three fume drakes are present in the hot spring water on the island
the fume drakes serve as guardians of the hot spring on the island
the fume drakes become hostile and attack if players gather mushrooms or enter the hot spring water
stranded on the island after a shipwreck and now considers it its territory
hostile toward characters as intruders in its territory, though aims to drive them away rather than kill
kobolds are hiding in rocks and light foliage around the island, attempting to ambush travelers
winged kobold participates in the ambush alongside regular kobolds
kobolds reject the peaceful teachings of Runara
kobolds prey on travelers who stray away from Dragon's Rest
a group of kobolds attempts to ambush the characters, fighting to the death unless offered money or food
winged kobold participates in the ambush against the characters
Characters might investigate Sharruth's tomb as part of an adventure
Characters might scour the island to find hidden vents and access tunnels
Tarak used to trade with the myconids in Seagrow Caves, exchanging food scraps for rare fungi
Sinensa is the leader of the myconid colony living in Seagrow Caves
Sinensa leads the myconid colony in Seagrow Caves
Noxious fumes from Sharruth's tomb are poisoning the myconids in Seagrow Caves due to a blocked vent
Players must face stirges that nest in Seagrow Caves, which can be deadly in large numbers
Dragon's Rest has a rowboat the characters can take
Tarak's preferred method of travel is by rowboat
The characters arrive at Seagrow Caves at sea level
defends themselves if the characters attack first
flee toward the nearest adults to warn them of intruders
is the leader of the myconid sprouts
normally avoid the crystal cave due to sunlight but inhabit the caves
live in the Seagrow Caves with their leader Sinensa
lurks in the water of the tunnel in the Seagrow Caves
attacks any creatures (other than myconids) who enter the tunnel, including players
created by the myconid leader to keep outsiders away from the tunnel
two Stirge join the battle if fighting breaks out in the area
Three violet fungus grow among the harmless mushrooms in the cave
If characters move toward the myconids, one violet fungus attacks with rot-causing tendrils
characters kill the violet fungi with ranged attacks while staying out of their reach
The Myconid Sprout avoid the characters
The Myconid Adult move quickly to protect the sprouts when aware of danger
After characters defeat violet fungi, adults agree to accompany and vouch for them with the colony
can make each heart cap mushroom into a potion of healing
Six stirges emerge from a nest in the chamber when characters enter
Sprouts rely on stirge distraction to escape toward area B4 when intruders appear
tends to the unconscious leader by collecting spores from a glowing red fungus and puffing them around Sinensa's head to keep them alive
uses ruby morel to create the elixir of health
Two Fume Drakes lurk amid the sulfurous fumes in the cave and don't leave it
The presence of the red dragon Sharruth deep beneath the island causes the volcanic activity that produces the noxious fumes in the cave
released from the broken fire crystal and immediately attacks the characters, seeing them only as fuel
emerges from the broken fire crystal in the cave and begins attacking
Sinensa gives the players permission to keep treasure and mushrooms after they help destroy the orange crystal
residents urge the players to visit the wreck of Compass Rose next
settled into the wreck of Compass Rose and uses it as its lair
lures ships to crash, creating new zombies from drowned sailors
shambling zombies from shipwrecks haunt the island's shores
menaces the people of Dragon's Rest after escaping the harpy
some zombies have been trapped in the wreck for years
can be found aboard sunken ships as one of the dangers characters might encounter
can be found aboard sunken ships as one of the dangers characters might encounter
The characters can tie up the rowboat to the south (starboard) side of the shipwreck's rigging
climb the rope ladder up the mast to the crow's nest, leaning out over the water on the port side
is out looking for another ship to lure onto the rocks near the shipwreck
two small tiger eye gems found in the crow's nest of the cursed shipwreck
one bloodstone gem found in the crow's nest of the cursed shipwreck
found in one of two small drawers in a desk in the ship's hold
found in one of two small drawers in a desk in the ship's hold
found in one of two small drawers in a desk in the ship's hold
The players are exploring the ship's hold where they observe the galley and skeleton
The fine clothes are worn by the blond-haired man in the portrait found on the cursed shipwreck
A sack full of 200 gp rests in a small compartment beneath the raised floorboard in the ship's hold
is more cunning than the zombies and uses them as a distraction
waits to attack characters when they're busy with the zombie, hoping to paralyze and drag one away
A crowbar can be used to pry open crates in the ship's hold
A fine lute with mother-of-pearl inlay can be found in one of the crates
A spell scroll of command sealed in a leather case can be found in one of the crates
three turquoise gems are inside the pouch
contained within the iron chest on the floor of the hold
The players' journey ended near Neverwinter docks after the shipwreck
Many Undead creatures like Ghoul worship Orcus, the Demon Prince of Undeath
lands on the main deck above the hold where the players are located
confronts the characters on the main deck and uses Luring Song to charm them on its first turn
can convince the harpy to leave through diplomacy, Charisma checks, or specific tactics
In a dream, the players see Stormwreck Isle shrouded in darkness, then a ray of sunlight breaks through and they feel the love and approval of their god
battled centuries ago, with Sparkrender being a descendant of Runara's ancient rival
has made the observatory ruins his home in recent months
overpowered Aidron and imprisoned him in the observatory ruins
imprisoned in the observatory ruins by Sparkrender
was under Runara's tutelage at Dragon's Rest before leaving
holds Aidron prisoner as the key to unlocking the observatory's magic
The players are deciding how to travel to the Clifftop Observatory from Dragon's Rest
The characters approach the observatory by land, eventually arriving at area D1
are locked in battle with eight stirges at the clifftop observatory
if the characters actively help the kobolds during the battle, the kobolds return the favor
if the characters attack the kobolds instead of or as well as the stirges, the kobolds fight back
Mek and Minn are brothers of Myla but left her for dead after a stirge attack that maimed her wings
are present at the clifftop observatory where the battle with stirges occurs
are present at the clifftop observatory where they battle the winged kobolds
if friendly, offer to help retrieve the moonstone key from area D1 to activate the bridge to D4
Three kobolds named Ekrash, Erp, and Hev currently inhabit the camp at the clifftop observatory
Kobolds are polishing sling bullets and keeping busy until Sparkrender's ritual
Winged kobolds are polishing sling bullets and keeping busy until Sparkrender's ritual
found together in the hidden compartment, with the potion likely placed inside or alongside the pouch
contains 10 gp that can be taken by the players, implying a reward or loot for their investigation
found by the players as a result of their successful investigation check, serving as a reward
Bronze Dragon Wyrmling (Aidron) is imprisoned in the secret library below the observatory (area D6)
Players can free Bronze Dragon Wyrmling (Aidron) to gain his help in fighting Sparkrender
Players can choose to fight Sparkrender the blue dragon wyrmling, either with or without Aidron's help
can potentially wake and fight the dragon if stealth checks fail
Sparkrender becomes hostile and attacks if characters mention Runara
five pale blue quartz crystals are part of Sparkrender's hoard at the clifftop observatory
five blue jasper stones are part of Sparkrender's hoard at the clifftop observatory
Aidron the bronze wyrmling asks for help defeating Sparkrender and will return to Dragon's Rest with them after victory
asks the characters to help him defeat Sparkrender, explaining the blue dragon's plan to use his death to become a draconic avatar
can be found by players who succeed on a DC 15 Intelligence (Investigation) check while searching the library
can be found by players who succeed on a DC 15 Intelligence (Investigation) check while searching the library
described in appendix A and can be found in the library
described in appendix A and can be found in the library
Runara possesses potions of healing that she gives to the characters as rewards
Runara possesses exquisite pearls that she gives to the characters as rewards
